title: Dutch Defense: Leningrad
summary: Learn the aggressive Dutch Defense, where Black fights for kingside space and attack.
description: The Dutch is an aggressive defense where Black aims for early kingside pawn play and attack. You'll learn the Leningrad Dutch, a modern interpretation that combines solidity with attacking chances.
line: 1.d4 f5 2.Nf3 Nf6 3.g3 g6 4.Bg2 Bg7 5.O-O O-O 6.c4 d6
objectives: Understand the Dutch idea: claim kingside space and prepare a pawn storm.
Learn typical Dutch plans: ...f4, ...e5, or ...d5 depending on the structure.
Master the art of playing for initiative with limited material.
plans: White can play quietly with Nc3 and Bg5, or go for a direct attack.
Black seeks to play ...f4 and ...e5, creating central pressure and kingside threats.
The Dutch is sharp: both sides have aggressive options.
commonMistakes: Do not play too passively. The Dutch gives Black active piece play.
Avoid ignoring the kingside. That's where the battle is.
Do not underestimate Black's attacking chances. They are real.
completionSummary: The Dutch Defense creates dynamic positions where both sides must play accurately.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.coaching: The queen's pawn opening. White controls the center before Black's pawn move.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: The Dutch Defense
steps.instruction: Play f5, claiming kingside space and controlling e4.
steps.coaching: This is the Dutch: Black fights for kingside space and prepares to attack.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. The knight controls e5 and supports the center.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6, attacking e4.
steps.coaching: Standard development. The knight controls e4 and prepares ...g6.

steps.title: Prepare kingside development
steps.instruction: Play g3, preparing to fianchetto the bishop.
steps.coaching: This is a common setup: White plays positionally and prepares kingside development.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: Play g6, preparing to develop the bishop to g7.
steps.coaching: Black prepares the Leningrad setup: bishop on g7 for kingside pressure.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.coaching: The bishop develops to g2, controlling the center and long diagonal.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to g7, mirroring White's setup.
steps.coaching: Both sides have similar setups. The position is balanced but tense.

steps.title: White castles
steps.instruction: Castle to get the king safe and connect the rooks.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.instruction: Black castles, getting the king safe.
steps.coaching: Both kings are now safe. The real battle begins with piece development.

steps.title: Expand on the queenside
steps.instruction: Play c4, controlling more central space.
steps.coaching: White expands on the queenside while Black pushes on the kingside.

steps.title: Black prepares development
steps.instruction: Play d6, supporting the e5 square and preparing piece development.
steps.coaching: Black prepares to develop pieces while maintaining kingside pressure.
